page: Amazon Web Services m7a.medium is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features a single dedicated physical core and vCPU from an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. However, due to its single-vCPU configuration, multi-core and parallelized workloads perform poorly, as evidenced by bottom-tier scores in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is mixed, showing average performance for large block writes but poor results for small block cached reads. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong, while database operations and static web serving throughput fall into the bottom 10%.

The m7a.medium offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that require dedicated CPU execution without the virtualization overhead of shared-core instances. The primary tradeoff is the strict limitation of a single vCPU and the lack of local storage, which restricts its utility for multi-threaded applications, heavy database operations, or high-throughput web serving. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, utility scripting, and small-scale development or testing environments.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring a single dedicated vCPU from an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System, it includes 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%, alongside strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, its single-vCPU architecture results in poor multi-core, database, and web-serving performance. This instance represents a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without shared-core virtualization overhead. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and development or testing environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring a single dedicated AMD EPYC vCPU, 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.medium is a general-purpose instance with 1 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPU and 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, ideal for single-threaded tasks.
tagline: A single-core, dedicated AMD EPYC instance with DDR5 memory, delivering strong single-threaded performance for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz
4.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio)
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and development environments
